subject: Add instrumentation layer
description:
Metrics capture - what to track and why.

Walk the repo first: read CLAUDE.md and fundforge.md instrumentation section.

Work:
- Page view tracking: page name, timestamp, referrer, viewport size (fires on every route change)
- Conversion events: donate button click (with campaign ID, amount), share click, follow click
- Web Vitals: LCP, FID/INP, CLS per page (use web-vitals library or Performance API)
- Error tracking: window.onerror / unhandledrejection with page context and session UUID
- Engagement: scroll depth on campaign page (25/50/75/100%), time on donation panel
- Analytics output: structured console logging with event type, timestamp, payload (or a simple /analytics route that displays captured events)
- Generate a session UUID on first visit, persist in sessionStorage

The "explain what metrics you'll capture and why" requirement means the architecture doc needs a rationale for each metric. Capture notes as you build.

Write back to CLAUDE.md:
- Event taxonomy (list of all event types with their payloads)
- Why each metric matters (brief rationale)
- How to view captured metrics (console, route, etc.)

changes: Created app/src/lib/analytics.js - event bus with session UUID, structured console logging, Web Vitals, error tracking
Created app/src/lib/useAnalytics.js - React hooks for page views and scroll depth
Updated main.jsx to init error tracking + Web Vitals on startup
Updated CLAUDE.md with full event taxonomy table
learnings: web-vitals auto-code-splits to 5.8KB chunk via dynamic import
Scroll depth uses a Set for dedup so milestones only fire once per campaign per session
